quiz_questions.question_text: Which axis is the horizontal axis on a graph?

options.text: The y-axis
The x-axis
The z-axis
The t-axis

quiz_questions.explanation: The x-axis is the horizontal axis that runs left to right along the bottom of a graph. The y-axis is the vertical axis that runs up and down on the left side.

quiz_questions.question_text: What should every scientific graph include?

options.text: A title, labeled axes with units, and plotted data points
Only a title and a single labeled axis with no data points
Colorful decorations, borders, and artistic illustrations
Only plotted data points without any labels or title

quiz_questions.explanation: A proper scientific graph must include a descriptive title, both axes labeled with variable names and units, and accurately plotted data points. These elements help anyone reading the graph understand the data.

quiz_questions.question_text: What type of graph uses bars to compare categories of data?

options.text: A line graph
A pie chart
A bar graph
A scatter plot

quiz_questions.explanation: A bar graph uses rectangular bars of different heights or lengths to compare quantities across categories. Bar graphs are ideal when comparing separate groups, such as the population of different cities.

quiz_questions.question_text: What type of graph is best for showing how data changes over time?

options.text: A pie chart
A bar graph
A circle graph
A line graph

quiz_questions.explanation: A line graph connects data points with lines and is best for showing trends or changes over time. For example, tracking temperature changes throughout a day is best displayed on a line graph.

quiz_questions.question_text: On a graph, where does the independent variable typically go?

options.text: On the y-axis (vertical)
On the x-axis (horizontal)
In the graph title only
Inside the data table only

quiz_questions.explanation: By convention, the independent variable (the one the scientist controls or changes) is plotted on the x-axis (horizontal), and the dependent variable (the one that responds) is plotted on the y-axis.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does a straight line going upward on a graph indicate?

options.text: The variables have no relationship to each other
As one variable increases, the other also increases
As one variable increases, the other one decreases
The experiment had errors during data collection

quiz_questions.explanation: An upward-sloping line indicates a positive relationship: as the x-variable increases, the y-variable also increases. For example, as study time increases, test scores may increase.

quiz_questions.question_text: Why is it important to start the axes of a graph at zero?

options.text: To save space on the paper when drawing the graph
To avoid misrepresenting the data and showing accurate proportions
To make the graph look more colorful and interesting overall
It is not important; axes can start at any random value

quiz_questions.explanation: Starting axes at zero prevents visual distortion of the data. If a graph does not start at zero, small differences can appear much larger than they actually are, which can be misleading.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a best-fit line on a scatter plot?

options.text: A line connecting every single data point on the graph
A line drawn through the general trend of the data points
A line drawn along the x-axis at the bottom of the graph
A line connecting only the first and last data points plotted

quiz_questions.explanation: A best-fit line (or trend line) is drawn through the middle of scattered data points to show the general trend. It does not need to touch every point — it represents the overall pattern in the data.

quiz_questions.question_text: What type of graph shows parts of a whole as slices?

options.text: A line graph
A scatter plot
A circle (pie) graph
A bar graph display

quiz_questions.explanation: A circle graph (also called a pie chart) divides a circle into slices to show how parts relate to the whole. Each slice represents a percentage of the total. For example, it can show what fraction of the atmosphere is nitrogen, oxygen, etc.

quiz_questions.question_text: What should you include with axis labels on a scientific graph?

options.text: The units of measurement for each variable
Only the numbers without any additional text
The name of the student who collected data
Only the variable name without any units

quiz_questions.explanation: Axis labels must include both the variable name and its units of measurement (for example, 'Temperature (°C)' or 'Distance (m)'). Without units, the numbers on the graph have no physical meaning.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a scatter plot?

options.text: A graph that uses individual data points to show the relationship between two variables
A graph that uses bars of different heights to compare categories of items
A graph that uses slices of a circle to show percentages of a total value
A graph that uses connected lines to display only one variable over time

quiz_questions.explanation: A scatter plot displays individual data points on a coordinate grid to show the relationship between two variables. Patterns in the scattered points reveal whether the variables are related and how.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is interpolation on a graph?

options.text: Estimating a value between two known data points on a graph
Predicting a value far beyond the range of collected data
Removing data points that do not match expected results
Drawing the axes and title before plotting any data points

quiz_questions.explanation: Interpolation means estimating a value that falls between two known data points. For example, if you know the temperature at 2 PM and 4 PM, you can interpolate to estimate the temperature at 3 PM.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is extrapolation on a graph?

options.text: Extending the trend line to predict values beyond the collected data range
Finding the average of all the data points that were plotted on the graph
Removing incorrect data points from the graph before drawing the line
Estimating a value that lies between two known measured data points

quiz_questions.explanation: Extrapolation means extending a trend line beyond the range of collected data to predict future or unknown values. It is less reliable than interpolation because conditions may change outside the measured range.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the scale of a graph axis?

options.text: The title written at the very top of the graph display
The range and intervals of numbers marked along an axis
The color used to draw the lines on the graph paper
The type of graph selected to display the collected data

quiz_questions.explanation: The scale of an axis refers to the range of values displayed and the intervals between markings. For example, an axis might go from 0 to 100 with intervals of 10. Choosing an appropriate scale ensures data fills the graph area well.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'trend' mean when analyzing a graph?

options.text: The general direction or pattern that the data shows over time
A single unusual data point that does not fit with the rest
The label written on the x-axis of a data graph display
The exact numerical value of any specific plotted data point

quiz_questions.explanation: A trend is the general direction or pattern shown by data on a graph. Trends can be increasing (going up), decreasing (going down), or flat (staying constant). Identifying trends helps scientists draw conclusions.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a positive correlation between two variables?

options.text: When one variable increases while the other variable decreases
When both variables increase or decrease together in the same direction
When there is no relationship at all between the two variables
When both variables remain constant and do not change at all

quiz_questions.explanation: A positive correlation means both variables change in the same direction — when one increases, the other increases too. On a graph, this appears as an upward-sloping pattern from left to right.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a negative correlation between two variables?

options.text: When both variables increase together in the same direction
When one variable increases as the other variable decreases
When both variables stay the same and do not change at all
When the data contains errors that make the graph unreliable

quiz_questions.explanation: A negative correlation means the variables change in opposite directions — as one increases, the other decreases. On a graph, this appears as a downward-sloping pattern from left to right.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is an outlier on a graph?

options.text: The point that lies exactly on the best-fit line of the graph
A data point that falls far from the general pattern of the other points
The first data point that was plotted on the graph during analysis
The highest value recorded during the entire experiment process

quiz_questions.explanation: An outlier is a data point that does not fit the general pattern of the rest of the data. Outliers can result from measurement errors, unusual conditions, or natural variation.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'slope' mean on a line graph?

options.text: The steepness of a line, calculated as the rise divided by the run
The total number of data points that are plotted on the graph
The point where the line crosses the horizontal x-axis of the graph
The label that describes what the y-axis represents on the graph

quiz_questions.explanation: Slope measures the steepness of a line and is calculated as rise (change in y) divided by run (change in x). A steeper line has a larger slope, meaning the y-variable changes more quickly.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'no correlation' mean on a scatter plot?

options.text: The data points are scattered randomly with no clear pattern between the variables
The data points form a perfectly straight line going from lower left to upper right
All the data points are clustered together in one small area of the graph
The data points form a perfectly straight line going from upper left to lower right

quiz_questions.explanation: No correlation means there is no visible pattern or relationship between the two variables. The data points appear randomly scattered across the graph, and knowing one variable does not help predict the other.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student collects data on the temperature outside every hour from 8 AM to 4 PM. Which type of graph should she use to display this data?

options.text: A pie chart showing temperature percentages
A line graph showing temperature over time
A bar graph comparing temperature categories
A scatter plot with no connecting lines

quiz_questions.explanation: A line graph is the best choice for showing how a continuous variable (temperature) changes over time. The connected points make it easy to see the trend throughout the day.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student graphs the height of a plant each week for 6 weeks. The graph shows a straight line going upward. What can she conclude?

options.text: The plant grew at a steady rate over the six-week period
The plant stopped growing after the second week of observation
The plant grew faster at the end than at the beginning period
The plant grew quickly at first and then slowed down later

quiz_questions.explanation: A straight upward line on a graph indicates constant, steady growth. The plant increased in height by the same amount each week. If growth had sped up or slowed, the line would curve.

quiz_questions.question_text: A class survey finds that 40% of students prefer soccer, 30% prefer basketball, 20% prefer swimming, and 10% prefer tennis. Which graph type best displays this data?

options.text: A line graph tracking preferences over the school year
A scatter plot showing each student's choice plotted
A circle (pie) graph showing each sport as a slice
A histogram grouping students by their age ranges

quiz_questions.explanation: A circle (pie) graph is ideal for showing parts of a whole as percentages. Since the survey data shows what fraction of students prefer each sport (adding up to 100%), a pie chart displays this most clearly.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student plots distance vs. time for a moving car. The line curves upward, getting steeper over time. What does this indicate about the car's motion?

options.text: The car is moving at a constant speed without changing
The car is slowing down gradually as time passes
The car is speeding up as time goes on during the trip
The car is stopped and not moving at all during this time

quiz_questions.explanation: A curve that gets steeper over time on a distance-time graph means the car covers more distance in each time interval — it is speeding up (accelerating). A straight line would mean constant speed.

quiz_questions.question_text: A scientist graphs the relationship between hours of sunlight and plant growth. She notices one data point far above all others. What should she do?

options.text: Delete the point immediately because it must be a mistake in measurement
Identify it as a possible outlier and investigate before removing it
Move the point closer to the other data points so the graph looks better
Change the scale so the point appears to fit with the rest of the data

quiz_questions.explanation: An unusual data point should be identified as a possible outlier and investigated. The scientist should check if a measurement error occurred or if there is a valid explanation before deciding whether to keep or exclude it.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student's graph shows the number of ice cream cones sold each month. The data peaks in July and drops in December. What relationship does this suggest?

options.text: Ice cream sales have no connection to the time of year whatsoever
Ice cream sales increase during warmer months and decrease in colder months
Ice cream sales remain constant throughout every month of the year
Ice cream sales are highest during the coldest months of the winter season

quiz_questions.explanation: The graph shows a seasonal pattern: sales peak in July (summer) and drop in December (winter). This suggests that warmer temperatures are associated with higher ice cream sales.

quiz_questions.question_text: A teacher asks students to graph the masses of five different rocks. Which type of graph would be most appropriate?

options.text: A line graph connecting the five rock masses together
A bar graph with one bar for each rock sample tested
A scatter plot with the rock masses plotted over time
A pie chart dividing the total mass into five slices

quiz_questions.explanation: A bar graph is best for comparing separate categories (each rock). A line graph would not be appropriate because the rocks are distinct objects, not a continuous series. Each bar shows the mass of one rock.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student creates a graph but forgets to label the y-axis. Why is this a problem?

options.text: It makes the graph look slightly less colorful and attractive
Readers cannot tell what variable or units the y-axis represents
It causes the data points to shift to wrong positions on the graph
The graph will automatically be marked as invalid by computers

quiz_questions.explanation: Without a label and units on the y-axis, the reader has no way to know what the numbers represent. The data becomes meaningless — are the values measuring temperature, distance, mass, or something else entirely?

quiz_questions.question_text: A student's scatter plot shows data points that form a downward slope from left to right. Her partner says this shows a positive correlation. Is the partner correct?

options.text: Yes, any visible pattern in the data means it is a positive correlation
Yes, a downward slope always represents a strong positive correlation
No, a downward slope represents a negative correlation between variables
No, a downward slope means there is no correlation between the variables

quiz_questions.explanation: A downward slope from left to right shows a negative correlation — as one variable increases, the other decreases. A positive correlation would slope upward from left to right.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student graphs the speed of a ball rolling down ramps of different heights. The graph shows a curved line that gets steeper. What does this tell the student about the relationship?

options.text: The relationship between ramp height and ball speed is linear and constant
Increasing ramp height has no effect on the speed of the rolling ball
The ball speed increases more rapidly as the ramp height gets greater
The ball speed decreases as the ramp height increases over the range

quiz_questions.explanation: A curve that gets steeper indicates a non-linear relationship where the ball's speed increases more and more as ramp height grows. Each increase in height produces a larger increase in speed than the one before it.
